rag_rules: Use AGREE logical operators (and, or, not, =>) for branching logic
Place output assignments in implementation scope only when the left-hand side is an existing output feature or declared auxiliary variable
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations (e.g., `0.0 -> pre(t) + step`)
Use case-provided thresholds and legal visible feature names in contracts
Use AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) for declarations
generic_rules: Use nested if ... then ... else for branching logic
Place assumptions and guarantees in valid component type scopes
Use assign only for implementation contracts with existing output features or auxiliary variables
Use concrete bounds, named constants, or visible relations for input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `assume "label": expression;` and `guarantee "label": expression;` for contracts
Use `eq name : type = expression;` for equations and `const name : type = value;` for constants
Use `assign output = expression;` for output assignments in implementation scope
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through variables or outputs
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants
Do not use AGREE scalar type names (integer, boolean) in declarations
irrelevant_topics: AADL property names are not AGREE variables
AGREE type mismatch between assumed and actual types
